summary: Applied Compose runtime secret injection via OpenBao Agent on docker-runtime-lv3 for Windmill, Keycloak, Mattermost, Open WebUI, NetBox, the platform-context API, and the mail-platform gateway, and verified that each migrated stack now consumes `/run/lv3-secrets/<service>/runtime.env` instead of legacy compose-directory `.env` files.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest pytest -q tests/test_compose_runtime_secret_injection.py` passed with 8 assertions covering shared OpenBao Agent injection, runtime payload construction, sidecar runtime settings, and the mail gateway image contract.
`scripts/validate_repo.sh compose-runtime-envs health-probes` passed after the compose-runtime env guard and authenticated mail-platform readiness probe contract were updated on `main`.

Runtime-only converges against `docker-runtime-lv3` completed successfully for Windmill, Mattermost, Open WebUI, NetBox, the platform-context API, Keycloak, and the mail platform after fixing the OpenBao Agent privilege-drop behavior, the mail gateway image build, and the authenticated readiness probe contract.

Host verification on `docker-runtime-lv3` confirmed non-empty `/run/lv3-secrets/{windmill,keycloak,mattermost,open-webui,netbox,platform-context,mail-platform}/runtime.env` files, no remaining legacy compose-directory `.env` files for the migrated stacks, and healthy OpenBao Agent sidecars for each service.

notes: The live apply surfaced four repo defects that were corrected before the final green run: self-referential Windmill and Mattermost runtime-secret payload construction, the OpenBao Agent image dropping from root to the internal `openbao` user before reading root-owned AppRole files, the mail gateway image omitting `telemetry.py` from the build context, and a mail-platform readiness probe that targeted an authenticated endpoint without supplying the gateway API key.

A stale third-party apt source on `docker-runtime-lv3` (`/etc/apt/sources.list.d/influxdata.list`) blocked the first converge because `https://repos.influxdata.com/debian stable` no longer served Release metadata. It was manually disabled on the live VM during this apply as `/etc/apt/sources.list.d/influxdata.list.disabled-adr0077-20260323` so repo-managed apt tasks could proceed.
